### Post by xyz on 2007-03-18
Hello everyone,

So when I opened this file...
> # HTTP Cookie File
# [http://www.netscape.com/newsref/std/cookie_spec.html](http://www.netscape.com/newsref/std/cookie_spec.html)
# This is a generated file!  Do not edit.
# To delete cookies, use the Cookie Manager.

.ubuntuforums.org	TRUE	/	FALSE	1177970519	__utmz	234146522.1162202519.8.4.utmccn
which looks like the above quote, lots of lines like those. And this despite deleteing cookies in FF.

Can I safely delete this file despite the 'Do not edit' warning? I don't want just anyone
using this computer to have such a simple-easy-to-find access to the sites I visit.
Thanks for reading.

---

### Post by oilchangeguy on 2007-03-18
in firefox go to edit, preferences, from there you can change the default settings. and have them automatically delete when firefox is closed.

---

### Post by xyz on 2007-03-18
Thnx for the reply but I know about that.
Even when I go > Preferences > Privacy and delete cookies by hand, they still show in my 'cookies.txt' which is located in my profile....
